items.title: Anthropic Closes $65B Series H at $965B Valuation, Surpasses OpenAI
items.details: Anthropic raised $65 billion in a Series H round on May 28, 2026, led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Sequoia, Capital Group, Coatue, D1 Capital, GIC, ICONIQ, and XN. The $965B post-money valuation makes Anthropic the world's most valuable private AI company, overtaking OpenAI's $852B mark. Anthropic's run-rate revenue surpassed $47B as Claude becomes central to global enterprise deployments.

items.title: SpaceX Files S-1, Targets $75B Raise in Historic June IPO
items.details: SpaceX filed its public S-1 with the SEC on May 20, 2026, disclosing $18.67 billion in consolidated 2025 revenue after its February 2026 all-stock acquisition of xAI. The company is targeting a Nasdaq debut under ticker SPCX around June 12, with Goldman Sachs leading, at a valuation between $1.75 trillion and $2 trillion — which would surpass Saudi Aramco's $26B listing as the largest IPO in history. Up to 30% of shares are allocated to retail investors.

items.title: OpenAI Reports $5.7B Q1 2026 Revenue, Targets Fall IPO
items.details: OpenAI generated approximately $5.7 billion in Q1 2026 revenue — about $1 billion ahead of Anthropic's $4.8B for the same period — driven by ChatGPT subscriptions, enterprise tools, and AI coding products like Codex. OpenAI is preparing to confidentially file for a U.S. IPO targeting a September listing near a $1 trillion valuation, with Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley advising. The company projects $14 billion in losses for full-year 2026, with cash-flow breakeven not expected until 2029.

items.title: Anthropic Projects First-Ever $559M Operating Profit in Q2 2026
items.details: Anthropic is projecting its first-ever quarterly operating profit of approximately $559 million in Q2 2026, on revenue of at least $10.9 billion — more than double its Q1 figure. Sacra estimates Anthropic hit $45B in annualized revenue in May 2026, up from $9B at end-2025, driven by enterprise API contracts. By contrast, OpenAI faces a projected $14B annual loss for 2026 and a breakeven target pushed to 2029.

items.title: Q1 2026 Global VC Sets All-Time Record: $300B with AI Taking 80%
items.details: Q1 2026 saw $300 billion in global venture investment across 6,000 startups — an all-time record according to Crunchbase — with AI accounting for $242 billion or 80% of total funding. Four mega-rounds alone — OpenAI ($122B), Anthropic ($30B), xAI ($20B), and Waymo ($16B) — collectively raised $188 billion, or more than 63% of total quarterly funding. European venture funding also reached $17.6B in Q1 2026, up nearly 30% year-over-year, with AI claiming over 50% of Europe's total for the first time.

items.title: OpenAI Closes $122B Round at $852B Valuation with Amazon as Exclusive Cloud Partner
items.details: OpenAI closed what is now the single largest private venture round in history at $122 billion on March 31, 2026, pushing its post-money valuation to $852 billion. Amazon was named the exclusive third-party cloud partner as part of a $50 billion commitment. The round was co-led by SoftBank, Andreessen Horowitz, and D.E. Shaw Ventures, with Nvidia and SoftBank each investing $30 billion, and for the first time $3 billion was raised from retail investors through bank channels.

items.title: White House Releases National AI Policy Framework, Targets State Law Preemption
items.details: On March 20, 2026, the White House released its National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence, outlining legislative recommendations to establish a unified federal approach and limit what it views as burdensome state-level AI regulation. The framework is paired with Sen. Marsha Blackburn's 291-page TRUMP AMERICA AI Act discussion draft, seeking to codify executive AI orders and constrain state authority. The framework prioritizes child safety, innovation sandboxes, and federal preemption while explicitly avoiding new federal rulemaking bodies.

items.title: AI M&A Hits 266 Deals in Q1 2026 — 90% Year-over-Year Surge
items.details: CB Insights reported 266 AI M&A deals closed in Q1 2026, a 90% increase year-over-year, with acquisitions concentrated in data infrastructure, LLM Ops, and AI security. Landmark deals included ServiceNow's ~$3 billion acquisition of Moveworks for NLP-powered enterprise workflow automation and Workday's $1.1 billion deal for Swedish AI startup Sana. EY data shows corporate deals above $100M rose 65% in value and 17% in volume in the February–April 2026 period versus the same period in 2025.

items.title: Global AI Market Forecast: $539B by End-2026, $3.4T by 2033
items.details: The global AI market, valued at $390.91 billion in 2025, is expected to reach $539 billion by end-2026 per multiple analyst estimates, with Statista placing the figure at $617.62 billion on a broader definition. Technavio projects the AI market will grow by over $310 billion from 2026–2030 at a CAGR of 26.9%. Morgan Stanley Research estimates nearly $3 trillion of AI-related infrastructure investment will flow through the global economy by 2028, with more than 80% of that spending still ahead.

items.title: Hyperscaler AI Capex Consensus Reaches $527B for 2026
items.details: Goldman Sachs reports the Wall Street consensus for 2026 hyperscaler AI capital expenditure now stands at $527 billion, up from $465 billion at the start of Q3 2025 earnings season, continuing a trend of upward revisions. Cumulative 2025–2027 hyperscaler capex is projected at $1.15 trillion — more than double the $477 billion spent 2022–2024. Goldman Sachs warns that justifying this capex would require an annual AI profit run-rate exceeding $1 trillion, more than double the $450B 2026 consensus income estimate.

items.title: Enterprise AI Adoption Hits 72%; Generative AI Usage Doubles to 65%
items.details: McKinsey's 2026 Global AI Survey finds 72% of enterprises now run at least one AI use case in production, up from 55% in 2024. Generative AI adoption doubled from 33% in 2024 to 65% in 2026, accelerated by falling model costs and proven ROI. IDC projects total global AI spending will reach $301 billion in 2026, up from $223 billion in 2025, with Gartner estimating AI software alone will account for $157 billion.

items.title: Shield AI Raises $1.5B Series G at $12.7B Valuation for Defense Autonomy
items.details: Shield AI secured $1.5 billion in Series G funding as part of a broader $2.25 billion capital package, co-led by Advent International and JPMorgan Chase, with $500M from Blackstone preferred equity, valuing the company at $12.7 billion — up 140% in just one year. The startup's Hivemind autonomous pilot platform was selected by the U.S. Air Force for its Collaborative Combat Aircraft program, the first time mission autonomy software has been decoupled from the aircraft itself. Shield AI projects $540M+ in 2026 revenue, representing 80% year-over-year growth.

items.title: Pentagon Signs AI Deals With OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, SpaceX
items.details: The Pentagon announced AI contracts with OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and SpaceX to deploy large language models on classified Department of Defense networks for lawful operational use. The DoD's GenAI.mil platform, launched in December with Google Gemini, has already been used by more than 1.3 million personnel who have generated tens of millions of prompts and deployed hundreds of thousands of AI agents in five months. The DoD's 2026 budget request includes $33.7 billion earmarked for science, technology, and autonomous systems.

items.title: Microsoft-OpenAI Exclusivity Ends; OpenAI Now Multi-Cloud
items.details: A restructured April 2026 deal between Microsoft and OpenAI eliminated Microsoft Azure's exclusive hosting rights for OpenAI models. OpenAI can now serve all its products to customers across AWS, Google Cloud, Oracle, and any other infrastructure partner it chooses. Microsoft retains approximately 27% stake under the October 2025 recapitalization, but a renegotiated revenue-share agreement caps total payments at $38 billion through 2030, with roughly $6 billion owed in 2026 alone.

items.title: NVIDIA Posts $216B Annual Revenue; Announces Meta Multiyear GPU Partnership
items.details: NVIDIA reported $216 billion in annual fiscal 2026 revenue, with Q1 FY2027 revenue hitting $44.1 billion — up 69% year-over-year — driven by data center demand. NVIDIA announced a multiyear, multigenerational strategic partnership with Meta spanning on-premises, cloud, and AI infrastructure including deployment of millions of NVIDIA Blackwell and Rubin GPUs. NVIDIA also unveiled its Rubin platform, comprising six new chips delivering up to a 10x reduction in inference token cost versus the Blackwell platform.

items.title: Generative AI Market Forecast: $83.3B in 2026, Hitting $988B by 2035
items.details: Global Market Insights forecasts the generative AI market will grow from $53.7 billion in 2025 to $83.3 billion in 2026, expanding at a 31.6% CAGR to reach $988.4 billion by 2035. OpenAI led with over 23.6% market share in 2025, while the software segment accounted for 81% of market revenue. Cloud deployment held 73.8% market share in 2025, driven by scalability and seamless integration into enterprise digital ecosystems.

items.title: DOJ and FTC Escalate AI Antitrust Actions on Algorithmic Pricing
items.details: The DOJ and FTC have taken active enforcement positions in cases involving algorithmic pricing and information-sharing allegedly facilitated by AI systems, according to Morgan Lewis. Several class-action lawsuits have been filed in the U.S. challenging use of AI to coordinate pricing strategies. California's AB 325 (Preventing Algorithmic Price Fixing Act), effective January 1, 2026, strengthens antitrust oversight by prohibiting the use of AI-driven 'common pricing algorithms' among competitors and lowers the pleading standard for civil claims under the Cartwright Act.

items.title: Cognition AI Raises $1B Series D at $26B Valuation as Devin Writes 89% of Code
items.details: Cognition AI raised over $1 billion in Series D funding co-led by Lux Capital, General Catalyst, and 8VC, with participation from Founders Fund and Ribbit Capital, more than doubling the company's valuation in under eight months to $26 billion. The Devin autonomous coding agent now writes 89% of Cognition's own production code, validating autonomous software engineering as an operational reality. Earlier data showed Cognition's ARR surged from $1 million in September 2024 to $73 million by June 2025, leading to a prior $10.2 billion valuation.

items.title: Legora Raises $550M Series D at $5.55B Valuation for Legal AI
items.details: Legora raised $550 million in a Series D round at a $5.55 billion valuation, led by Insight Partners with participation from Index Ventures, IVP, Bessemer Venture Partners, and Vine Ventures, bringing total funding to nearly $1 billion. The collaborative AI platform helps lawyers and in-house legal teams research cases, review documents, and draft filings more efficiently. The round coincided with Legora's aggressive U.S. market expansion, cementing legal AI as one of 2026's deepest-funded professional services categories.

items.title: Enterprise AI ROI Gap Widens: 97% Report Benefits but Only 29% See Significant Returns
items.details: A 2026 survey by WRITER found 79% of organizations face challenges adopting AI — a double-digit increase from 2025 — with 54% of C-suite executives admitting AI adoption is causing structural organizational strain. Despite near-universal benefit claims, only 29% of organizations see significant ROI from generative AI, even as individual productivity gains are real. Median time-to-ROI has dropped from 24 months in 2024 to 14 months, and AI governance spending has grown to 8–12% of AI budget in 2026, up from 3–5% in 2024.
